name: 3body_conservation_law_search
description: Search for approximate conservation laws in the circular restricted 3-body problem (CRTBP) beyond the known Jacobi constant, and in near-resonant hierarchical triples. Bruns (1887) and Poincaré (1889) prove no additional *exact* algebraic or analytic integrals exist in the general 3-body problem. The open space is: (1) approximate integrals valid on secular timescales, (2) resonant action-like quantities near mean-motion resonances, (3) conserved structures in specific periodic orbit families (figure-8, choreographies). A candidate passes if it survives both the oracle (positive margin vs. plausible wrong forms) AND the conservation checker (numerical d/dt ≈ 0 along integrated trajectories). Oracle-fail + checker-pass is the most interesting outcome: it means we found something the training data didn't know.

discovery_criteria.oracle_pass: positive margin on >= 75% of verification facts
discovery_criteria.novelty: not a function of the 10 classical integrals alone
discovery_criteria.interesting_case: formal_pass=true AND oracle_fail=true (model hasn't seen it)

context: Bruns 1887: no additional algebraic integrals. Poincaré 1889: no additional analytic integrals in general. Open: approximate integrals near resonances, CRTBP configurations, choreographic orbits. Known CRTBP integral: Jacobi constant C_J = n^2(x^2+y^2) + 2(mu1/r1 + mu2/r2) - v^2. Hierarchical triple secular integrals: averaged orbital elements (a, e, i) approximately conserved on timescales >> orbital period.
